_spec.purpose: Self-improvement ledger (confidence-scored lessons). Every run's retro appends 1-3 instincts learned from: editor/critic flags, machine QA catches, human edits observed in later Gmail drafts, or performance notes the maintainer adds. Before each run, the orchestrator injects the TOP 5 instincts with confidence >= 0.7 into the directors-room and copy prompts. Confidence rises +0.05 each run that confirms an instinct, falls -0.1 each run that contradicts it. Prune entries below 0.5 confidence that are older than 8 runs.

entries.instinct: Plan every 3D camera with the horizonY arithmetic in ak3d.js before rendering; never eyeball camera placement.
entries.evidence: Two consecutive smoke-test terrain renders buried the mountain range in the bottom 15% of frame until the projection math was computed explicitly. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: slide 4 planned the low horizon at an explicit y and used parallax planes, not eyeballed camera; monumental result

entries.instinct: Apply grain as a small repeating tile (AK.grainTile), never a full-frame feTurbulence rect.
entries.evidence: Full-frame SVG grain rasterized to a 34MB incompressible bitmap in the printed PDF; the tile version produced 6.7MB. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: AK.grainTile used on all 9 slides; PDF 10.7MB, no incompressible full-frame grain

entries.instinct: Never treat a machine_qa PASS as composition approval; the pixel critics judge composition, hierarchy, and collision at full size and thumb size.
entries.evidence: A slide with the terrain crammed into the bottom 15% and a stat row colliding with the ridge passed every objective gate. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: pixel/scorer caught 4 DOM collisions (slide 3 overlap hard fail, slide 8 door graze, slide 9 coastline, slide 2 crowding) that machine_qa PASSED | CONFIRMED 2026-07-09: machine_qa PASSED (WARN) but pixel critics caught the S4 '12 fish' claim being uncountable, the S5 unblurred foreground (DOF), and the S6 dishonest 0-year nub bar -- all composition/honesty, not objective. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-11: critics caught ~10 canvas-vs-DOM overlaps + low-contrast in-section labels that machine_qa PASSED (S2 legend/homes, S3 curve labels, S4 readouts on ochre, S6 SUSPENDED, S7 flag mash, S8 context on ochre). | CONFIRMED 2026-07-13: pixel critics caught a favored honesty panel (S3), a bottle-read turbine (S4), and a strikethrough ledger (S7) that machine_qa PASSED clean.

entries.instinct: Zoom a d3 map by scaling a baseline full-state fitExtent and re-translating onto the target point, drawing the coastline STROKE-ONLY; never fitExtent to a small lon/lat bbox polygon.
entries.evidence: Slides 2 and 7 first used fitExtent to a bbox polygon and rendered a giant near-black fill disc plus a mis-scaled mini-Alaska with overlapping labels; the manual scale+translate with stroke-only coast fixed both cleanly.

entries.instinct: Before rendering, sanity-check long serif/body copy line counts against any fixed-position labels, bars, or plates; DOM text overlaps pass machine QA but fail the eye.
entries.evidence: Slide 3 body wrapped to 4 lines and overlapped the 'URBAN ALASKA GRID PEAK' bar label (a scorer-caught hard fail); slide 5 quote 4 slid under the Boyle plate. Both passed machine_qa safe-zone checks. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-09: four headline-wrap collisions (S1/S4/S7/S8) all traced to a display headline in a container narrower than its longest line; each machine_qa-FAILED as a text overprint. | CONFIRMED 2026-07-11: S2/S3/S4 body blocks overran their planned line counts into the section; capping max-width so the body ENDS in the sky zone fixed all three.

entries.instinct: Put data-decorative on the leaf text element (span, b, or the appended SVG <text>), not just the parent div; the QA tiny-text gate does not inherit it.
entries.evidence: annot <div data-decorative> still flagged its child <b>/<span> readouts on slides 2/4/7 until the attribute was added to each leaf element.

entries.instinct: Set a display headline's container to the full width its longest line needs (or drop the size) and verify the rendered line count before shipping; a too-narrow container silently wraps into the body and fails the overprint gate.
entries.evidence: S1 (98px in 952px), S4/S7 (78-80px in ~460px), S8 (94px in 920px) each wrapped an extra line into the block below and machine_qa-FAILED as a collision until widened or downsized.

entries.instinct: Budget the bottom third of every slide deliberately: a data readout, a caption, an anchor, or an intentional quiet zone -- do not let it default to dead empty space the scorer reads as thin craft.
entries.evidence: Scorer (8.83) capped Artwork Craft at 7 partly for 'large empty lower thirds' on several slides (S2, S6, S7, S8) even though every slide passed gates and critics.

entries.instinct: Canvas-drawn art can overprint DOM text without tripping qa.py (it only checks DOM line boxes); route every canvas path/arc/diagram around the text blocks at PLAN time and have pixel critics explicitly check canvas-vs-text collision.
entries.evidence: S3 flightpath arc crossed two body lines and S4 orbit crossed the headline; machine QA passed both, pixel critics caught both. | Re-confirmed run 5 (S5 loop arcs vs block plates; S8 body vs closed ring) both caught by pixel/flow critics, invisible to qa.py.

entries.instinct: Give the deck's continuity motif explicit connective tissue on EVERY slide (enter-left/exit-right at low alpha), not just its transformation states; intermittent motifs read as separate images.
entries.evidence: Flow critic: conductor trace read on only ~6 of 9 frames until S3/S6/S7 connective strokes were added.

entries.instinct: Keep top-strip kickers short (<=18 chars) when a right-aligned readout shares the row; long kickers visually abut the readout even when boxes do not intersect.
entries.evidence: S2/S7/S8 kickers crowded the readout at full width and were shortened.

entries.instinct: Any in-section DOM readout or canvas label over a light strata/ochre band must ride an explicit dark plate (CSS background plate for DOM, canvas knockout rect for canvas text); light mono on ochre measured ~1.5:1 and failed the eye though qa.py PASSED.
entries.evidence: Pixel critics failed S4 readouts, S8 context line, S3 curve labels, S6 SUSPENDED tag for contrast on the warm ochre band; a .plate-dark CSS class + canvas knockout chips fixed every one.

entries.instinct: Cap each body block's max-width so it resolves to at most its planned line count and ENDS inside the sky zone (above the section art) at PLAN time, then verify against the surface line; bodies silently overrun into the canvas art region and collide (qa cannot see canvas-vs-text).
entries.evidence: S2 (4 lines vs 3), S3, S4 (6 lines) each overran into homes/labels; widening the measure so the body ends by ~y640 cleared the collisions.

entries.instinct: For a state-change motif carried by ONE object (valve open->shut, switch on->off), change the object's GEOMETRY unmistakably (an amber lock-bar across the valve hub), not just a rotation; a subtle rotation of a radial/symmetric object reads identical at 432px.
entries.evidence: Flow critic: the 8-spoke valve looked near-identical rotated; the open->shut turn relied entirely on the SUSPENDED tag until an amber lock-bar across the hub made 'shut' read at thumb.

entries.instinct: A discrete-state motif must show its progress with an EXPLICIT quantized cue (a fraction like N/4, or clearly dim-vs-bright segments), not brightness alone; faint-cyan-vs-bright-cyan arc increments read as a static logo at corner/thumb size (flow critic, run 5).
entries.evidence: Run 5's Autonomy Ring: per-stage arc increments were illegible until unlit arcs were made gray and an explicit N/4 label was added under the ring.

entries.instinct: When two warm elements must read as the SAME encoding (e.g. two 'Alaska = fire' bars), pin them to one shared token and verify AFTER akpost; a yellowish orange (#FF6A2C, high green channel) drifts toward gold under the grade and both clashes with the split bar and strains the gold budget. Use a redder fire-orange (#E8531F) for on-slide fire that must stay clearly non-gold.
entries.evidence: Run 5 slide 3: #FF6A2C lit ISOTYPE bars read gold after grade; #E8531F matched the split bar and cleared the gold budget.

entries.instinct: A rendered akthree object hero reads as a flat blob at thumb unless a warm RIM light (opposite the key) separates it from the background; a pure backlit silhouette de-risks lumpiness but loses recognizability. Add a low rim from the key-light direction and slightly enlarge so the object's profile (rotors/arms) resolves.
entries.evidence: Run 5 slide 6: backlit-only drone read as a smear (flow critic); adding a 1.7-intensity warm rim + scale 1.15->1.42 made it read as a quadcopter.

entries.instinct: On a two-source honesty/firewall slide (an attributed third-party claim vs the primary source's own words), give BOTH panels identical visual weight -- same top, min-height, plate opacity, and accent-glyph treatment. A deliberate y-offset or size difference reads as favoritism and inverts the slide's honesty purpose.
entries.evidence: Run 6 S3: the left panel (the News-Miner's data-center demand line) rendered larger/brighter than the right (GVEA's own 'higher load' words); the pixel critic flagged the imbalance as favoring the attributed framing. Equal tops + min-height + matched quote glyphs fixed it.

entries.instinct: When DOM text must share a slide with a full-bleed GPU/akthree render, guarantee legibility with a Deep-Sea-Scrim DOM gradient (dark to transparent) behind the text column, layered above the canvas -- not text-shadow, and not by shrinking the object off its composition. text-shadow alone fails over bright specular metal.
entries.evidence: Run 6 S4: body copy over the rendered LM6000's steel dome dropped below 4.5:1 (pixel-critic BLOCKER) despite a text-shadow; a left-column dark-to-transparent scrim div made it fully legible while keeping the hero object big.

entries.instinct: Fixed-width, centered in-scene label DIVs trip the safe-zone gate on their BOX edges even when the glyphs sit well inside the margin. Size in-scene label containers to content (white-space:nowrap + translateX(-50%)) so the box, not just the text, stays within the 80px margins.
entries.evidence: Run 6 S2: a 220px-wide centered node-label box at left:815 pushed its right edge past the 1000px safe margin though '2ND UNIT' was short and centered; switching to content-width nowrap boxes cleared the warn.
